graded_findings.ref:
Today there is no way
to check that an edited cluster config is well-formed before a deploy begins
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence marks this requirement as addressed by the spec, so it is useful coverage evidence but not a merge blocker.

graded_findings.ref:
A missing or empty `worker_nodes` list is only discovered mid-deploy, after
   `start` has begun contacting hosts.
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence marks this requirement as addressed by the spec, so it is useful coverage evidence but not a merge blocker.

graded_findings.ref:
An out-of-range or non-integer `worker_port` surfaces as an opaque failure
   deep in the worker launch rather than as a clear config error.
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence marks this requirement as addressed by the spec, so it is useful coverage evidence but not a merge blocker.

graded_findings.ref:
Missing SSH credentials (`ssh_auth.ssh_user_name` / `ssh_private_key`) fail
   late during multi-node fan-out instead of up front.
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence marks this requirement as addressed by the spec, so it is useful coverage evidence but not a merge blocker.

graded_findings.ref:
Operators need a fast, read-only `dscli` command that validates a cluster config
file and reports every problem at once, so misconfigurations are caught before
any deploy action runs.
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence marks this requirement as addressed by the spec, so it is useful coverage evidence but not a merge blocker.

graded_findings.ref:
reports each structural problem (missing/empty required fields, wrong types,
out-of-range port), returning a non-zero exit code when the config is invalid.
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence marks this requirement as addressed by the spec, so it is useful coverage evidence but not a merge blocker.

examined:
Today there is no way
to check that an edited cluster config is well-formed before a deploy begins

A missing or empty `worker_nodes` list is only discovered mid-deploy, after
   `start` has begun contacting hosts.

An out-of-range or non-integer `worker_port` surfaces as an opaque failure
   deep in the worker launch rather than as a clear config error.

Missing SSH credentials (`ssh_auth.ssh_user_name` / `ssh_private_key`) fail
   late during multi-node fan-out instead of up front.

Operators need a fast, read-only `dscli` command that validates a cluster config
file and reports every problem at once, so misconfigurations are caught before
any deploy action runs.

reports each structural problem (missing/empty required fields, wrong types,
out-of-range port), returning a non-zero exit code when the config is invalid.
